title: SOP for Resolving Core Router High Latency in NYC Datacenter (#121) - mainframe-host-01
summary: The Core Router High Latency issue in the NYC Datacenter (#121) is causing network connectivity problems, including high latency and potential timeouts. The root cause is related to network connectivity issues, specifically with the border router (router-border-nyc-01) and its peering with AWS East Region. To resolve the issue, Network Ops needs to investigate router logs, BGP peering, and path latency to isolate and fix the problem.
symptoms: High latency in network traffic passing through the core router in NYC Datacenter
Potential timeouts and connection issues with AWS East Region services
rootCause: The root cause of the issue is likely related to network connectivity problems between the border router (router-border-nyc-01) and AWS East Region, potentially due to misconfigured BGP peering, incorrect routing tables, or high latency in the network path.
resolutionSteps: Investigate router logs and BGP peering configurations to identify potential issues with the border router (router-border-nyc-01) and its connectivity to AWS East Region
Analyze network path latency and routing tables to determine if there are any misconfigurations or bottlenecks causing high latency

Collaborate with Network Ops team to implement fixes, such as updating BGP peering configurations, optimizing routing tables, or implementing Quality of Service (QoS) policies to prioritize critical traffic
author: 🤖 Meta Llama 3.3 70B Instruct (NVIDIA NIM)

title: Troubleshooting & SOP: Printer Spooler Offline - London HQ Floor 3 (router-border-nyc-01)
summary: The incident involves synthesizing a KB article for a printer spooler issue resolved via database connection pool tuning and vacuum operations (Resolution Code: DB - Connection Pool & Vacuum). Despite the CI referencing a border router, the explicit database resolution code and analysis of 64 incidents indicate the root cause resides in the database layer, requiring DBA expertise for knowledge capture.
symptoms: Printer Spooler Offline - London HQ Floor 3 (#2) alerts triggered on router-border-nyc-01
System latency and intermittent connection timeouts reported in diagnostic logs.
rootCause: The incident involves synthesizing a KB article for a printer spooler issue resolved via database connection pool tuning and vacuum operations (Resolution Code: DB - Connection Pool & Vacuum). Despite the CI referencing a border router, the explicit database resolution code and analysis of 64 incidents indicate the root cause resides in the database layer, requiring DBA expertise for knowledge capture.
resolutionSteps: 1. Inspect configuration item status on router-border-nyc-01 using diagnostic CLI commands.

2. Apply recommended fix: KB synthesis task: Document printer spooler offline symptom (London HQ Floor 3) linked to DB connection pool exhaustion and vacuum maintenance. Root cause: PostgreSQL connection pool saturation during peak print jobs; resolved via pgbouncer config tuning and scheduled vacuum analyze. Attach query plans and pool metrics from incidents KB0000001-KB0000064.
3. Verify service health metric baseline and confirm ticket resolution.
author: 🤖 Agentic AI Knowledge Synthesizer (NVIDIA Nemotron 3 550B)
